title: Dupatya fastigiata
taxonomy: Dupatya fastigiata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the genus Dupatya, which belongs to the family Celastraceae. The family Celastraceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the staff vine family or the bittersweet family. This family consists of around 900 species and is widely distributed across the world. The genus Dupatya is native to tropical regions of Asia, with Dupatya fastigiata being a specific species within this group. The classification of Dupatya fastigiata is further detailed in botanical literature, where it is often included in the list of species within the Celastraceae family.
uses: Dupatya fastigiata, also known as the Bastard Boa or Fastigiate Dupatya, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits.
